title: Foma Gordeyev
author: Maxim Gorky
description: A merchant's son inherits a Volga shipping empire but, repelled by his class's greed, descends into drunken rebellion — Gorky's first novel and a landmark of Russian realism about the spiritual cost of capitalism.

title: Mother
author: Maxim Gorky
description: A widowed factory worker is slowly awakened to revolutionary consciousness by her Bolshevik son's activism, becoming one of literature's great portraits of political conversion and maternal courage.

title: The Lower Depths
author: Maxim Gorky
description: Destitute vagabonds — thieves, prostitutes, a dying woman, a drunken actor — crowd a Moscow flophouse and debate truth, illusion, and human dignity in Gorky's masterpiece of naturalist drama.

title: My Childhood
author: Maxim Gorky
description: The first volume of Gorky's autobiographical trilogy recounts a brutal, impoverished boyhood in a dye-works household in Nizhny Novgorod, where cruelty and tenderness existed side by side.

title: In the World
author: Maxim Gorky
description: The second autobiography follows the young Gorky as he works a succession of grinding jobs — dish boy, icon painter's apprentice, dockhand — and begins his voracious self-education through books.

title: My Universities
author: Maxim Gorky
description: The trilogy's concluding volume traces Gorky's years among Volga radicals and Kazan's intellectual underground, the 'university' of poverty and political awakening that forged the writer he became.

title: The Artamonov Business
author: Maxim Gorky
description: Three generations of a Russian industrialist family rise and collapse between the emancipation of the serfs and the 1917 Revolution, a sweeping dynastic chronicle often called Gorky's finest novel.

title: The Life of Klim Samgin
author: Maxim Gorky
description: A vast four-volume chronicle following an individualist Russian intellectual from the 1870s through the Revolution of 1917 — Gorky's magnum opus and one of the great novels of the twentieth century.

title: Petersburg
author: Andrei Bely
description: On the eve of the 1905 Revolution, a senator's son is handed a bomb and ordered to kill his own father — Bely's hallucinatory modernist masterpiece, ranked by Nabokov alongside Ulysses and The Metamorphosis.

title: The Silver Dove
author: Andrei Bely
description: A young Russian poet becomes dangerously enmeshed with a rural religious sect, the Silver Doves, in Bely's symbolist precursor to Petersburg — a hypnotic study of mysticism, eroticism, and doom.

title: Kotik Letaev
author: Andrei Bely
description: Told from inside an infant's emerging consciousness, this experimental novel reconstructs the bewildering, mythic world of early childhood through streams of imagery and incantatory prose.

title: Dangerous Liaisons
author: Choderlos de Laclos
description: Two aristocratic libertines — the scheming Marquise de Merteuil and the Vicomte de Valmont — wage a cold-blooded epistolary war of seduction and revenge that destroys everyone in its path.

title: Indiana
author: George Sand
description: A young Creole woman trapped in a loveless marriage defies social convention to pursue passion, making Sand's debut novel a landmark protest against laws that bound women to their husbands against their will.

title: Valentine
author: George Sand
description: An aristocratic young woman falls for a peasant farmer, and their love tests the rigid class barriers of rural France in this early, tenderly observed novel of romance and social inequality.

title: Lélia
author: George Sand
description: A deeply introspective, almost plotless philosophical novel in which a disillusioned woman rejects love, religion, and society — scandalous on publication for its frank treatment of female desire and spiritual despair.

title: Mauprat
author: George Sand
description: A rough, brutalized young man raised by a family of brigands is transformed by his love for his noble cousin Edmée — Sand's thrilling blend of Gothic romance, historical adventure, and Rousseauian philosophy.

title: Consuelo
author: George Sand
description: A gifted Venetian singer navigates love, artistic ambition, and the courts of eighteenth-century Europe — Sand's grandest novel and, for Walt Whitman, her supreme achievement.

title: The Countess of Rudolstadt
author: George Sand
description: Consuelo's story continues as she is drawn into the secret Freemasonic world of Frederick the Great's Prussia — a sweeping novel of Enlightenment idealism, conspiracy, and revolutionary hope.

title: The Devil's Pool
author: George Sand
description: A young widower is guided by an old peasant to woo a humble shepherdess rather than a wealthy rival — a perfect pastoral idyll that opens Sand's celebrated series of Berry country tales.

title: François the Waif
author: George Sand
description: An abandoned foundling grows up amid the mills and farms of Berry; his gentle nature wins him love and belonging in Sand's most warmly humane pastoral novel.

title: Little Fadette
author: George Sand
description: A sharp-tongued, unconventional country girl nicknamed Fadette wins the heart of one of a pair of devoted twin brothers — the last and most charming of Sand's three great rural Berry novels.

title: The Master Pipers
author: George Sand
description: A young apprentice bagpiper travels through the Berry hills learning the ancient mysteries of his craft and confronting questions of loyalty, love, and artistic vocation.

title: She and He
author: George Sand
description: A thinly veiled autobiographical account of Sand's turbulent love affair with Alfred de Musset, framed as a novel and notable for its unflinching portrayal of a woman's creative independence within a consuming passion.

title: Horace
author: George Sand
description: Set among Paris students during the July Monarchy's social unrest, Sand's politically sharpest novel dissects the vanity of a charming provincial egotist against a backdrop of working-class struggle.

title: A Kiss for the Leper
author: François Mauriac
description: An ugly, sickly young man and a beautiful woman are pressured into a joyless provincial marriage — Mauriac's breakthrough novella, a stark Catholic meditation on sacrifice, repulsion, and spiritual grace.

title: Génitrix
author: François Mauriac
description: A possessive mother's stranglehold on her adult son and his frail young wife leads to tragedy in this merciless, compressed portrait of provincial bourgeois cruelty set in the Landes pine forests of southwest France.

title: The Desert of Love
author: François Mauriac
description: A father and his teenage son both fall silently in love with the same enigmatic woman, and seventeen years later the son encounters her again — Mauriac's luminous exploration of desire, shame, and missed connection.

title: Thérèse Desqueyroux
author: François Mauriac
description: A brilliant, suffocated woman poisons her dull husband, and then — acquitted and trapped — must face what drove her to it: Mauriac's most celebrated novel, a landmark of French psychological fiction.

title: Vipers' Tangle
author: François Mauriac
description: A dying miser pours his hatred of his devout family into a secret journal, only to find his bitter self-examination leading toward an unexpected spiritual reckoning — widely regarded as Mauriac's masterpiece.

title: The Frontenac Mystery
author: François Mauriac
description: Five orphaned siblings of a Bordeaux bourgeois family bind themselves in fierce mutual loyalty against the world, a warmly elegiac novel that stands apart from Mauriac's usual moral severity.

title: A Woman of the Pharisees
author: François Mauriac
description: A rigidly pious provincial woman wreaks quiet destruction on all around her — her stepson, a schoolmaster, a young couple — in Mauriac's devastating study of religious self-righteousness masquerading as virtue.

title: Under the Sun of Satan
author: Georges Bernanos
description: A tormented country priest wrestles with Satan in the Artois countryside while a teenage murderess hides her crime — Bernanos's explosive debut, a violent, visionary Catholic novel unlike any before it.

title: Joy
author: Georges Bernanos
description: Chantal, a young woman of radiant holiness, becomes a battleground between grace and evil in a corrupt household — a companion novel to Under the Sun of Satan and Bernanos's most luminous work.

title: The Diary of a Country Priest
author: Georges Bernanos
description: A young, sickly curé records his humiliations, failures, and flickers of grace in a hostile parish — Bernanos's undisputed masterpiece, one of the great spiritual novels of the twentieth century.

title: Mouchette
author: Georges Bernanos
description: A wretched fourteen-year-old girl in a brutish rural village meets rape and contempt at every turn until she finds a final, terrible dignity in death — an anguished novella of unbearable compassion.

title: Monsieur Ouine
author: Georges Bernanos
description: A dying, spiritually hollow retired professor presides over a disintegrating Norman village gripped by a child's murder — Bernanos's most enigmatic and experimental novel, written in exile during the war.

title: The Conquerors
author: André Malraux
description: During the 1925 Canton uprising, a group of revolutionary adventurers — including the enigmatic half-caste terrorist Hong — confront violence, ideology, and mortality in Malraux's electrifying debut political novel.

title: The Royal Way
author: André Malraux
description: Two men hack into the Cambodian jungle to loot forgotten Khmer temples and haul their prizes out through hostile terrain — an adventure novel that doubles as a meditation on death, will, and the lure of the absolute.

title: Man's Fate
author: André Malraux
description: During the 1927 Shanghai Communist uprising, a handful of revolutionaries face Chiang Kai-shek's massacre — the Prix Goncourt winner that established Malraux as a master of the political novel.

title: Days of Wrath
author: André Malraux
description: A Communist militant survives a Nazi prison through solidarity with an unknown comrade who takes his place — Malraux's shortest novel, a concentrated act of resistance art written before the world acknowledged the camps.

title: Man's Hope
author: André Malraux
description: Based on Malraux's direct experience fighting in Spain, this epic novel captures the chaos, heroism, and ideological tensions of the Spanish Civil War through a mosaic of soldiers and civilians on the Republican side.

title: Before the Storm
author: Theodor Fontane
description: In the winter of 1812–13, Prussian gentry and peasants debate whether to rise against Napoleon while their private lives unspool — Fontane's sprawling first novel and a masterpiece of nineteenth-century German historical fiction.

title: A Man of Honor
author: Theodor Fontane
description: A Prussian officer's rigid code of honor collides with desire and social scandal in this taut, ironic novella set in the Napoleonic era — Fontane at his most concentrated and quietly devastating.

title: Delusions, Confusions
author: Theodor Fontane
description: A Prussian officer and a working-class Berlin woman fall in love knowing that class difference makes marriage impossible — Fontane's most tender novel, an understated tragedy of social convention and genuine feeling.

title: Irretrievable
author: Theodor Fontane
description: A Schleswig-Holstein count and his wife drift apart until the gulf becomes unbridgeable, and a second chance at love ends in catastrophe — a quietly devastating novel of marital alienation.

title: Frau Jenny Treibel
author: Theodor Fontane
description: A coal merchant's wife preaches the nobility of feeling while scheming ruthlessly for money and status — Fontane's funniest novel, a razor-sharp satirical portrait of the German bourgeoisie at its most hypocritical.

title: Effi Briest
author: Theodor Fontane
description: A young woman married off to a much older Prussian official commits a brief indiscretion that destroys her life years later — Fontane's masterpiece, ranked alongside Madame Bovary and Anna Karenina as a great novel of adultery.

title: The Stechlin
author: Theodor Fontane
description: An elderly Prussian nobleman, his son, and their neighbors talk, visit, and debate the changing world in this leisurely autumnal novel — Fontane's last work, where the conversations are the drama and the ending is peace.

title: In the Land of Cockaigne
author: Heinrich Mann
description: A provincial opportunist claws his way into Berlin's decadent literary and social elite — Mann's satirical debut novel skewering Wilhelmine Germany's bourgeois corruption with acid wit and expressionist energy.

title: The Blue Angel
author: Heinrich Mann
description: A pompous small-town schoolmaster becomes obsessed with a cabaret singer called Lola, loses everything, and is destroyed — the source of the Marlene Dietrich film and Mann's most famous portrait of German authoritarian pride crumbling before desire.

title: The Little Town
author: Heinrich Mann
description: The arrival of an Italian opera troupe in a sleepy provincial town ignites passion, rivalry, and political upheaval — Mann's most affectionate novel and a warm-hearted celebration of art's power to shake complacency.

title: The Loyal Subject
author: Heinrich Mann
description: A bullying German bourgeois finds his perfect model in Kaiser Wilhelm II, mirroring imperial authority in every corner of his life — Mann's ferocious satirical masterpiece of Wilhelmine conformism.

title: Young Henry of Navarre
author: Heinrich Mann
description: The first volume of Mann's great historical diptych follows the young Henri of Navarre from his Protestant boyhood through the St. Bartholomew's Day Massacre as he grows into a leader of tolerance and pragmatic humanity.

title: Henry, King of France
author: Heinrich Mann
description: The second volume crowns Henri IV as king of a war-torn France and traces his reign of reason and religious reconciliation — Mann's tribute, from Nazi exile, to a ruler who chose humanity over ideology.

title: The Tanners
author: Robert Walser
description: Simon Tanner drifts through a series of jobs and encounters in turn-of-the-century Germany, perpetually cheerful and unwilling to settle — Walser's first major novel, a luminous portrait of principled vagrancy.

title: The Assistant
author: Robert Walser
description: Josef Marti goes to work as assistant to a failed inventor in a Swiss villa and observes the quiet tragicomedy of provincial ambition and genteel decline — Walser's most tender and socially observed novel.

title: Jakob von Gunten
author: Robert Walser
description: A young man of good family enrolls in a mysterious institute for servants, recording his daily life in a diary that becomes an eerily comic meditation on submission, identity, and the joy of self-erasure.

title: The Robber
author: Robert Walser
description: Written in Walser's tiny 'microscript' and published only posthumously, this radically digressive anti-novel follows a character called the Robber through a stream of whimsical, self-interrupting narrative that dissolves plot into pure literary play.
